source.name: Bundled AstroGuide target metadata overlay
source.notes: Combines curated nebula target metadata, curated galaxy target metadata, and target media correction overrides.

sourceCitations.notes: Huge southern H-alpha shell spanning Vela and Puppis; very wide-field target.
Faint, large PN; H-alpha/OIII are primary, SII usually weak.
Large Cygnus emission and dark-nebula field around Sadr.
Dark globule and rim in the larger IC 1396 H II region.
Strong SHO/narrowband target; Melotte 15 near the center.
Large emission complex adjacent to Heart Nebula.
Broadband reflection/dust target illuminated by Rigel.
Bright naked-eye southern open cluster; broadband star-color target with very faint reflection/dust structure.
Bright H II region around Lambda Centauri with Bok globules and embedded clusters; good SHO/HSO target.
Mixed red emission and blue reflection nebulosity.
Dust silhouette against the IC 434 H-alpha emission region.
Filamentary SNR; strong H-alpha and SII/OIII structures.
Part of the Monoceros R1 reflection-nebula complex.
Large broadband reflection nebula and dust field around Nu Scorpii.
Broadband reflection, dark dust, and molecular-cloud complex near Antares; H-alpha is localized rather than the main feature.
Large southern emission nebula in Scorpius; strong H-alpha/SII target with dust and weak OIII accents.
Adjacent to North America Nebula; rich dark dust lanes.
Bright mixed nebula at the end of Barnard 168 dark nebula.
Broadband dust target with two small blue reflection nebulae.
Large naked-eye dark nebula silhouetted against the rich southern Milky Way near Crux.
Dark molecular cloud often seen against faint Barnard's Loop emission.
Very large dark-nebula complex; best treated as a wide-field broadband dust target.
Dark, sinuous dust feature in the Ophiuchus cloud complex; broadband/dark-nebula target.
Bright supernova remnant with strong emission filaments and a broadband synchrotron component.
Includes the Pillars of Creation dark/dust structures.
Bright H II region also known as Omega or Horseshoe Nebula.
Classic mix of red emission, blue reflection and dark lanes.
Bright PN; OIII/H-alpha dominate, SII is usually secondary.
Dust lanes and H II regions; not a narrowband nebula target.
H-alpha from bright H II regions such as NGC 604.
Nearby NGC 2158 often included in the field.
Bright mixed nebula; strong H-alpha with dust and reflection components.
Broadband blue reflection dust around the cluster.
Classic bright annular planetary nebula; H-alpha/OIII are primary, SII usually secondary.
Compact planetary nebula with bright lobes and fainter outer extensions.
Common nickname for M78; broadband reflection and surrounding dust.
Large H II region with strong dust lanes and Hourglass area.
Face-on barred spiral with dust, star-forming arms, and optional H-alpha enhancement.
OIII/H-alpha target; SII signal is normally secondary.
Bright, resolved southern globular cluster and a strong broadband target.
Very bright southern globular cluster adjacent to the Small Magellanic Cloud in wide-field compositions.
Fornax Cluster showpiece with a strong bar, spiral arms, dust lanes, and optional H-alpha accenting.
Large H-alpha-rich nebula; SII usually secondary.
Primarily reflection/dust; red component is weak/atypical for H-alpha.
Tadpole-shaped dusty globules in an H II region.
Reflection-dominant target north of M42; H-alpha is secondary.
Reflection nebula with the distinctive keyhole-shaped dark void.

targets.curatedObjectTypeRaw: Emission nebula / giant H II region

sourceCitations.notes: Massive star-forming region in the Large Magellanic Cloud; very strong narrowband target for southern skies.
H II region associated with open cluster NGC 2175.
Large H II region around cluster NGC 2244.
Includes Cone Nebula, Christmas Tree Cluster and Fox Fur region.
Large emission complex across Monoceros/Canis Major.
Wind-blown shell around Wolf-Rayet star WR 7.
Bright compact planetary nebula with a detailed inner shell and fainter halo.
Large, low-surface-brightness planetary nebula; OIII/H-alpha are usually more useful than SII.
Large bright edge-on starburst galaxy; broadband target with dust lanes and optional H-alpha accenting.
Bright filament of the Vela SNR; compact narrowband target with sharp H-alpha/OIII/SII structure.
Contains embedded cluster IC 1590 and dark lanes.
Naked-eye satellite galaxy near 47 Tucanae; rich wide-field target with star clusters, dust, and compact emission regions.
Nearby Sculptor Group spiral; broadband target with diffuse arms and optional H-alpha accenting.
Compact southern planetary nebula with bright annular structure.
Bright compact planetary nebula; high-resolution OIII/H-alpha imaging works well.
Huge southern star-forming complex with bright emission, reflection components, dark lanes, and the Eta Carinae region.
Large bright open cluster in Carina; wide-field broadband target with many colorful stars.
Compact bright southern H II region with dust, pillars, and useful H-alpha/SII/OIII separation.
Small bright southern planetary nebula with strong blue-green OIII and useful Balmer-line signal.
Compact colorful open cluster next to the Southern Cross; strong broadband star-color target.
Large edge-on dusty galaxy near Centaurus A; broadband target with weak emission-line accent potential.
Famous galaxy with a prominent dust lane, bright stellar continuum, and star-forming regions in the disk.
Largest and brightest globular cluster in the sky; excellent broadband/luminance target.
Large edge-on Sculptor Group galaxy; broadband target with dust and patchy star-forming regions.
Dramatic bright-rimmed emission nebula around NGC 6193; strong narrowband target with dust lanes.
Bipolar planetary nebula with a dusty equatorial waist and strong line emission.
Large southern star-forming region with strong H-alpha/SII structure and embedded dust.

targets.preferredName: Lobster Nebula / War and Peace Nebula
aliases: Lobster Nebula / War and Peace Nebula

sourceCitations.notes: Southern H II region adjacent to Cat’s Paw; rich in H-alpha/SII filaments and dust.
Very compact bipolar planetary nebula; narrowband signal is useful but target scale is small.
Bright compact planetary nebula with a faint extended halo; high-resolution target.
ESO describes NGC 6559 as a Sagittarius star-forming nebula with glowing hydrogen gas, blue reflection nebulosity, and dark dusty clouds; common-name aliases curated manually.
Small bright planetary nebula with strong OIII and H-alpha components.
Likely the Sh2-54 'nest/egg' region north of M16/M17.
Large Milky-Way-like spiral in Pavo; broadband galaxy target with dust and faint H II regions.
Small planetary nebula; bright enough for high-resolution narrowband work.
Wind-blown shell around WR 136; strong narrowband target.
Large filamentary SNR; excellent H-alpha/OIII/SII target.
Shares the Sh2-117 complex with the Pelican Nebula.
Bright blue reflection core embedded in extensive dust.
Large nearby planetary nebula with visible knots and strong H-alpha/OIII structure.
Emission region surrounding open cluster NGC 7380.
Wind-blown bubble in surrounding H II region.
Bright compact planetary nebula with a small apparent size; high-resolution target.
Wide complex; Sh2-170 is often treated as the question-mark dot.

resolution.notes: No exact catalog identifier or alias match in bundled catalog.

sourceCitations.notes: Naked-eye satellite galaxy and very wide-field target with embedded H II regions, dust, clusters, and the Tarantula Nebula field.
H-alpha/SII target near Cygnus X-1.

targets.curatedObjectTypeRaw: O III bipolar nebula within H II region

sourceCitations.notes: Ou4 is OIII-dominant; H-alpha comes mainly from surrounding Sh2-129.
Wikimedia Commons identifies Flying Dragon Nebula as Sh2-113.
Sh2-119 is described as an emission nebula in Cygnus around 68 Cygni, with a shell-like form.
Large Cepheus H II region with associated dark nebulae.
Complex containing emission, reflection and dark nebulosity.
Large H II region near Bubble Nebula and M52.
Very large, faint filamentary SNR; benefits from long H-alpha/OIII/SII integrations.
Also imaged as Eye of Ra; SII adds structure in SHO data.
Large evolved planetary nebula; H-alpha and OIII are primary, with SII usually weak.
OIII-dominant wind bubble around WR 6; H-alpha and SII are usually secondary.

sourceCitations.notes: Large filamentary supernova remnant; strong narrowband target with H-alpha, SII, and OIII structure.
